summary: The actual screenshot depicts a detailed single-player lobby UI with map selection, commander name, server status, and opponent lineup table, aligning with the screen description. However, the wireframe is a simplistic and inaccurate representation, showing meta text and incorrect elements instead of the expected layout, resulting in significant mismatches.

correctElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: Dark starfield/game-map background
correctElements.evidence: Both actual and wireframe feature a dark starfield background.

correctElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: thin green panel borders
correctElements.evidence: Both show thin green borders around panels.
Both include 'Single Player Game Setup' as a prominent title.
Both have primary action buttons (e.g., START GAME vs. CREATE / CONTINUE) and back/cancel buttons.

incorrectElements.requirement: Screen description: Wide framed panel with map/name cards at top
incorrectElements.expected: Map selection card with label and dropdown/control.
incorrectElements.actual: Present in actual with 'Map Selection' label and 'Default' dropdown; absent in wireframe, replaced by descriptive text.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe does not depict the map card, preventing validation of this element.

incorrectElements.requirement: Screen description: Wide framed panel with map/name cards at top
incorrectElements.expected: Commander name input card with label and text field.
incorrectElements.actual: Present in actual with 'Commander Name' label and 'Player' input; absent in wireframe.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe lacks the name card, missing key setup element.

incorrectElements.actual: Present in actual; absent in wireframe.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe does not include server status, hindering validation.

incorrectElements.requirement: Screen description: scrollable opponent lineup table
incorrectElements.expected: Scrollable table with columns for commander, role, shuffle, assignment.
incorrectElements.actual: Detailed table present in actual with multiple rows; wireframe shows only descriptive text without table structure.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe fails to represent the table, a core component, as a visual element.

incorrectElements.actual: Actual has 'START GAME' and 'BACK'; wireframe has 'CREATE / CONTINUE' and 'BACK / CANCEL'.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Button labels do not match, potentially confusing validation of user actions.

incorrectElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: The output is a precise layout baseline, not a Mermaid flowchart
incorrectElements.expected: No meta or instructional text in wireframe.
incorrectElements.actual: Actual has no meta text; wireframe includes descriptive paragraph and 'Prompt baseline' label.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe contains extraneous text not part of the UI, violating precise layout baseline.

incorrectElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: fixed screen-relative geometry
incorrectElements.expected: Wide framed panel spanning screen with top cards, middle table, bottom actions.
incorrectElements.actual: Actual follows expected geometry; wireframe has stacked panels with incorrect positioning and no table.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe layout does not match described geometry, limiting spatial validation.

incorrectElements.requirement: FR-UI-AUDIT-001: Every documented UI screen must have a direct prompt-rendered wireframe at 1536x832 for screenshot comparison
incorrectElements.expected: Wireframe accurately renders described elements for comparison.
incorrectElements.actual: Wireframe is inaccurate, but actual matches description; residual risk of DPI/scaling differences not verifiable due to wireframe quality.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Even though actual appears correct, poor wireframe quality introduces uncertainty in pixel-perfect validation.

wireframeSuitability.why: The wireframe does not visually represent the key elements described in the screen description, such as the opponent lineup table, map and name cards, and server status. Instead, it displays the prompt text literally, making it ineffective for validating the functional and technical requirements.
missingRequirements: FR-UI-SCREEN-SINGLE-PLAYER-LOBBY: Visual satisfaction of screen description including table and cards
TR-UI-AUDIT-HWND-001: Accurate baseline for 1536x832 screenshot comparison
FR-UI-WIREFRAME-SUITABILITY-001: Suitability for validating screen requirements
recommendedWireframeChanges: Regenerate wireframe to visually depict map/name cards, server status, scrollable table, and precise action buttons without meta text.
Ensure adherence to global style: semi-transparent panels, terminal-style labels, simple rectangles.
Verify fixed geometry and include all described elements for precise comparison.
